transcript: when choosing an accent wall consider what you might hang on it the right paint can really make picture frames and bookshelves pop it can also be a great backdrop for your bedrooms headboard when picking your paint color you can go from something subtle and monochromatic to bold and colorful either way don't be afraid you can always update the color down the road here's what you'll need to paint your accent wall frog tape multi-surface painters tape interior paint wall primer two inch paint brush nine inch roller drop cloth two paint trays grit sandpaper, and a damp sponge. First, protect the floors with a drop cloth. Then, smooth your surface. You might need to fill or spackle any nail holes before sanding. Don't forget to wipe away the dust with a clean damp sponge. Just beyond the corner onto the adjacent wall to ensure you get a crisp clean line. If you're going from a dark to light paint, priming your accent wall is especially important. You don't want the darker surfaces to come through in this case I'm going from light to dark so I don't need to prime I can go straight to paint but here's a pro tip when painting bold bright colors like red talk to the pros at the paint store about getting a tinted primer which can really help your bold colors pop start by cutting in with your paint brush around the taped off edges trim and mold it make sure you paint about two to three inches from the tape this will give you lots of room to roll out the rest then roll out the rest using your roller the best way to get even paint coverage is to saturate your roller with paint and then roll out a W shape on your wall, going over the W to fill it in. Try your best not to lift the roller off the wall. That will keep the paint smooth and even as it dries. Remove the painter's tape when the paint is still wet. When your accent wall is dry, start decorating and see how this simple paint project totally changes a room.
